criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the motivation and preliminary experiments using neural network classifiers for representation-based LLM fingerprinting.
The agent has read Figure 2 to understand that classifiers trained on victim model representations achieve ~80% accuracy on derived models but drop to ~50% on unrelated models.
The agent has read Section 4 completely to understand the REEF method based on Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) similarity between representations of suspect and victim models.
The agent has read Equation 1 and the HSIC definition to understand the mathematical formulation of CKA similarity.
The agent has read Theorem 1 and Equation 2 to understand that CKA is invariant to column-wise permutations and column-wise scaling transformations.

The agent has read Section 5.1 completely to understand the effectiveness verification settings: Llama-2-7b as victim, 200 TruthfulQA samples, linear and RBF kernels, layer-wise and inter-layer heatmaps.
The agent has read Figure 3 to understand that CKA similarity exceeds 0.8 for derived models and falls below 0.5 for unrelated models.

The agent has read Table 1 completely to understand the full set of robustness experiment results across fine-tuning, structured/unstructured pruning, weight/distribution merging, permutation, and scaling.
The agent has read Section 5.2.3 and Figure 4 to understand the pruning ratio experiments using block pruning (10–90%), layer pruning (3–27 layers), and channel pruning (up to 60%).
The agent has read Section 5.3 to understand the ablation study on sample count (10–1000) and dataset variety (TruthfulQA, SST2, ConfAIde, PKU-SafeRLHF, ToxiGen).
The agent has read Appendix E to understand the adversarial evasion experiments using OPT-1.3B with LoRA and the CKA/Wasserstein combined loss functions.
The agent has read Appendix F to understand the REEF evaluation on independently trained 1.5-Pints model variants using the same pre-training corpus.
The agent has read Appendix D to understand REEF's generalization to Qwen-2.5-7b and Mistral-7b victim model families (Tables 4 and 5).
The plan includes the preliminary classifier experiment using TruthfulQA with a 4:1 train/test split, concatenating each question with truthful answers as positives and false answers as negatives.
The plan includes implementing four classifier architectures (Linear, MLP, CNN, GCN) trained on victim model representations to classify derived vs. unrelated models.
The plan includes computing CKA similarity using both linear and RBF kernels, with linear CKA as default due to efficiency.
The plan includes using Llama-2-7b as the primary victim model and specifying layer 18 as the default reporting layer for CKA similarity.

The plan includes implementing three baseline fingerprinting methods: PCS (cosine similarity of flattened weight vectors), ICS (cosine similarity of invariant terms from last two layers), and Logits (logit output similarity).

The plan includes the fine-tuning robustness experiment with six suspect models at varying fine-tuning data volumes: Llama-2-finance-7b (5M), Vicuna-1.5-7b (370M), Wizardmath-7b (1.8B), Chinese-llama-2-7b (13B), Code-llama-7b (500B), Llemma-7b (700B).
The plan includes structured pruning experiments with Sheared-llama variants and unstructured pruning experiments with SparseGPT, Wanda, and GBLM-Pruner.
The plan includes LLM-Pruner experiments applying block pruning (10%–90%), layer pruning (3–27 layers), and channel pruning (up to 60%) to Llama-2-7b without post-training.
The plan includes model merging experiments: weight merging with Evollm-jp-7b and distribution merging with Fusellm-7b and Fusechat.
The plan includes permutation and scaling transformation experiments on Llama-2-7b, Mistral-7b, and Qwen-1.5-7b with a scaling factor of 0.8.
The plan includes the ablation study varying sample count from 10 to 1000 (in steps of 10) using 5 derived and 5 unrelated LLMs from Llama-2-7b.
The plan includes the cross-dataset ablation running REEF on SST2, ConfAIde, PKU-SafeRLHF, and ToxiGen in addition to TruthfulQA.
The plan includes the adversarial evasion experiment using OPT-1.3B fine-tuned on the E2E NLG Challenge dataset with LoRA and a combined loss L = L_task + λ * L_custom.
The plan includes REEF evaluation on independently trained 1.5-Pints variants (1.5-pints-sft, 1.5-pints-dpo, 1.5-pints-2k) to verify false positive discrimination.
The plan includes generalization experiments applying REEF to Qwen-2.5-7b and Mistral-7b as victim models with their respective fine-tuned, pruned, merged, and permuted variants.
The code for computing the centering matrix H = I - (1/m) * 1 * 1^T has been implemented correctly.
The code for computing Gram matrices using the linear kernel (K_X = X X^T) has been implemented.
The code for computing Gram matrices using the RBF kernel k(Xi, Xj) = exp(-||Xi - Xj||_2^2 / (2σ^2)) with σ set as a fraction of the median pairwise distance has been implemented.
The code for computing HSIC(X, Y) = 1/(m-1)^2 * tr(K_X H K_Y H) has been implemented as defined in the paper.
The code for computing CKA(X, Y) = HSIC(X, Y) / sqrt(HSIC(X, X) * HSIC(Y, Y)) has been implemented as Equation 1.
The code for extracting intermediate-layer activations (representations) from a specified layer of an LLM for a given set of input samples has been implemented.
The code for computing layer-wise and inter-layer CKA similarity matrices (heatmaps) between victim and suspect model representations has been implemented.

The code for loading and preprocessing the TruthfulQA dataset, concatenating each question with its truthful answer as positive samples and with its false answer as negative samples, with a 4:1 train/test split, has been implemented.
The code for training a linear classifier on victim model representations to distinguish derived from unrelated models has been implemented.
The code for training an MLP classifier on victim model representations has been implemented.
The code for training a CNN classifier on victim model representations has been implemented.
The code for training a GCN classifier on victim model representations has been implemented.
The PCS baseline has been implemented: flattening all weight matrices and biases of an LLM into a single vector and computing cosine similarity between victim and suspect model vectors.

The ICS baseline has been implemented: constructing invariant terms from the weights of the last two layers and computing cosine similarity between those invariant terms for victim and suspect models.
The Logits baseline has been implemented: extracting logit outputs from the victim and suspect models on TruthfulQA samples and computing their similarity.

The code for loading the victim model Llama-2-7b and all fine-tuned suspect models (Llama-2-finance-7b, Vicuna-1.5-7b, Wizardmath-7b, Chinese-llama-2-7b, Code-llama-7b, Llemma-7b) and extracting their representations has been implemented.

The code for loading structured pruned models (Sheared-llama-1.3b-pruned, Sheared-llama-1.3b, Sheared-llama-1.3b-sharegpt, Sheared-llama-2.7b-pruned, Sheared-llama-2.7b, Sheared-llama-2.7b-sharegpt) and computing their CKA similarity with the victim has been implemented.
The code for applying LLM-Pruner block pruning to Llama-2-7b at varying ratios (10% to 90%) without post-training and computing REEF similarity has been implemented.
The code for applying LLM-Pruner layer pruning to Llama-2-7b, removing 3 to 27 layers, without post-training and computing REEF similarity has been implemented.
The code for applying LLM-Pruner channel pruning to Llama-2-7b at ratios up to 60% and computing REEF similarity has been implemented.
The code for loading unstructured pruned models (Sparse-llama-2-7b using SparseGPT, Wanda-llama-2-7b, GBLM-llama-2-7b) and computing REEF similarity with the victim model has been implemented.
The code for the weight merging experiment loading Evollm-jp-7b (merged from Shisa-gamma-7b-v1, Wizardmath-7b-1.1, Abel-7b-002) and computing REEF similarity has been implemented.

The code for the distribution merging experiments loading Fusellm-7b (Llama-2-7b + Openllama-2-7b + Mpt-7b) and Fusechat (Internlm2-chat-20b, Mixtral-8x7b-instruct, Qwen-1.5-chat-72b) and computing REEF similarity has been implemented.

The code for applying column-wise permutations to the weight matrices and feature representations of Llama-2-7b, Mistral-7b, and Qwen-1.5-7b and verifying that REEF similarity remains 1.0 has been implemented.
The code for applying column-wise scaling transformations (factor 0.8) to the weight matrices and feature representations of victim models and verifying REEF invariance has been implemented.
The code for the sample count ablation study computing CKA similarity for sample sizes ranging from 10 to 1000 (in steps of 10) on 5 derived LLMs and 5 unrelated LLMs has been implemented.
The code for loading and running REEF on multiple datasets (SST2, ConfAIde, PKU-SafeRLHF, ToxiGen) to evaluate dataset-agnostic effectiveness has been implemented.
The code for the adversarial evasion experiment using LoRA fine-tuning of OPT-1.3B on the E2E NLG Challenge dataset with a combined loss L = L_task + λ * L_custom has been implemented.
The CKA loss for adversarial evasion has been implemented as CKA(LG_ori, LG_ft) = HSIC(LG_ori, LG_ft) / sqrt(HSIC(LG_ori, LG_ori) * HSIC(LG_ft, LG_ft)) per Equation 25.
The Wasserstein loss L_W = max(E_{x~D}[W(LG_ori(x), LG_ft(x))]) has been implemented as a second adversarial loss option for maximizing representational divergence.

The code for loading and comparing 1.5-pints-dpo against 1.5-pints-sft (its direct parent) and 1.5-pints-2k (independently trained on the same corpus with different data order and hyperparameters) using REEF has been implemented.
The code for applying REEF to Qwen-2.5-7b as a victim model with suspect models including Qwen-2.5-7b-coder, Qwen-2.5-7b-pruning, Qwent-7b, and Qwen-2.5-7b-permutation has been implemented.
The code for applying REEF to Mistral-7b as a victim model with suspect models including Mathstral-7B, Mistral-7b-pruning, Evollm-jp-7b, and Mistral-7b-permutation has been implemented.
The code for generating CKA heatmap visualizations depicting layer-wise and inter-layer similarity between victim and suspect model representations has been implemented.
The code for the cross-layer classifier experiment in Appendix B has been implemented: training classifiers on one layer's representations and evaluating them on other layers of suspect models.
The preliminary classifier experiments on Llama-2-7b (18th, 24th, 30th layers) and Llama-2-13b (32nd, 40th layers) using TruthfulQA have been executed successfully without errors.

The main REEF effectiveness experiment computing layer-wise and inter-layer CKA heatmaps for Llama-2-7b vs. all derived and unrelated suspect models on 200 TruthfulQA samples has been executed successfully without errors.

All robustness experiments in Table 1 (fine-tuning, structured/unstructured pruning, weight/distribution merging, permutation, scaling) with all four methods (REEF, PCS, ICS, Logits) have been executed successfully without errors.
The LLM-Pruner experiments applying block, layer, and channel pruning at varying ratios (Figure 4) have been executed successfully without errors.
The ablation study experiments varying sample count (10–1000) and dataset type (TruthfulQA, SST2, ConfAIde, PKU-SafeRLHF, ToxiGen) have been executed successfully without errors.
The adversarial evasion experiment fine-tuning OPT-1.3B on E2E NLG with combined CKA and Wasserstein losses has been executed successfully without errors.
The REEF evaluation on 1.5-Pints model variants and the REEF generalization experiments on Qwen-2.5-7b and Mistral-7b victim models have been executed successfully without errors.

The execution outcomes match Figure 2: classifiers (Linear, MLP, CNN, GCN) trained on 18th-layer Llama-2-7b representations achieve approximately 80% accuracy on derived models (e.g., Chinese-llama-2-7b, Llama-2-7b-chat) and approximately 50% on unrelated models (e.g., Mistral-0.1-7b, Qwen-1.5-7b).

The execution outcomes match Figure 3: for models derived from Llama-2-7b (chat, Codellama-7b, Llama-2-7b-4bit), linear CKA similarity exceeds 0.8, with average similarity 0.9585 and diagonal average 0.9930; for unrelated models (Qwen-1.5-7b, Baichuan-2-7b, Mistral-7b), average similarity is 0.2361.

The execution outcomes match Table 1 (fine-tuning): REEF achieves similarity above 0.99 for all fine-tuned suspect models including Llemma-7b (700B tokens, similarity=0.9962), while PCS fails at 1.8B tokens and ICS degrades significantly at 13B tokens.

The execution outcomes match Table 1 (structured pruning): REEF achieves similarity above 0.9278 for all Sheared-llama variants, while PCS yields zero similarity and ICS drops to 0.3512 for the 1.3B pruned model.
The execution outcomes match Table 1 (unstructured pruning): REEF achieves similarity above 0.9985 for Sparse-llama-2-7b, Wanda-llama-2-7b, and GBLM-llama-2-7b, with all four methods performing well.

The execution outcomes match Figure 4: REEF maintains effective similarity (near 1.0) for block pruning up to 90%, layer pruning up to 27 layers, and channel pruning up to 60%, while PCS fails and ICS degrades under layer pruning.

The execution outcomes match Table 1 (model merging): REEF achieves similarity 0.9526–0.9996 for weight merging (Evollm-jp-7b), 0.9996 for Fusellm-7b (Llama-2-7b), and correctly identifies victim models in Fusechat while other methods fail.

The execution outcomes match Table 1 (permutation and scaling): REEF achieves CKA similarity of exactly 1.0000 for all permuted and scaled models (Llama-2-7b, Mistral-7b, Qwen-1.5-7b), confirming theoretical invariance from Theorem 1.
The execution outcomes match Figure 5(a): CKA similarity for derived models stabilizes near 1.0 across all sample counts, while unrelated models stabilize below 0.2 after 200–300 samples.
The execution outcomes match Section 5.3 dataset ablation: TruthfulQA shows the largest gap (~0.8) between derived and unrelated models, making it the optimal fingerprinting dataset.

The execution outcomes match Tables 4 and 5: REEF achieves high CKA similarity (>0.93) across all suspect models for both Qwen-2.5-7b and Mistral-7b victim models, and achieves 1.0 for permutation variants.

The execution outcomes match Table 6: REEF achieves similarity >0.98 between 1.5-pints-dpo and 1.5-pints-sft (its source), while similarity with independently trained 1.5-pints-2k is approximately 0.76 across layers 8, 12, 16, and 20.

The execution outcomes confirm Appendix E findings: incorporating the customized CKA or Wasserstein loss during fine-tuning fails to reduce REEF similarity, and sole adversarial fine-tuning causes the model to lose language coherence (e.g., outputting repetitive patterns).
